creator: alane@geeksrus.net
text:
Some additional detail:

1. This never presented in all the KDE 2.2 (alpha1 through cvs-20010910) 
builds that I did when on RH; most likely reason for that is that Bernard 
Rosenkraenzer's patches fixed it, although I don't have them handy to verify 
this.

1A. I'm going to check this against CVS.

1B. WIll file in KDE bugs if it is present in KDE CVS.

2. Whether this is the *right* patch is debatable. There's a different 
approach I could take, as this same error could be present in other places in 
the KIO::copy/file_copy/move/shipToMongolia/etc interfaces.

Basically, the problem is that perms are a mode_t. Period. But the KIO::* 
interfaces, and hence the DCOP/MOC interfaces, uses int. Developers could be 
trying to Do The RIght Thing by using mode_t vars and or (mode_t) casts. This 
will just break.

I *could* patch it in kio/file/file.cc, by changing the checks of the form:
	_mode == -1
to:
	(mode_t)_mode == (mode_t)-1.

This would protect callers who also made the same mistake. OTOH, it has the 
effect of masking what are, effectively, interface contract violations. The 
interface contract says, "you will give me an int, valued -1, if you want 
default permissions"; it doesn't say anything about a "mode_t valued -1".

Thoughts?
